mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO growth remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window, or the proportion of positive 20-day returns following INDPRO increases is not significantly above 0.5 (binomial test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.2 percentage points over the past 3 months signals labour market deterioration, reducing consumer confidence and future earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >= 0.2 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Conditional 20-day return following UNRATE rise triggers is not significantly negative (mean return >0 or t-test p>0.05), or hit rate of negative returns below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and stable or declining consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) compresses the real interest rate, lowering discount rates and boosting equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS declines and CPIAUCSL is stable-to-declining, the mean 20-day SPX return is not positive and significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labour market (UNRATE below 4%), a deceleration of payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) to below 150,000 per month signals weakening labor demand and late-cycle fragility, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Conditional on UNRATE<4% and PAYEMS growth <=150k, the mean 60-day SPX return is not negative or does not underperform the unconditional mean by at least 1% (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200,000 units per month signals weakening housing sector demand, which historically leads to broader economic slowdown and immediately depresses equity market sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day returns following HOUST below threshold show no significant negative bias (mean return >=0) or hit rate of negative returns below 55% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nominal retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for RSAFS monthly growth remains below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or the strategy of going long after positive RSAFS growth shows Sharpe ratio <0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) experience acceleration in their 3-month growth rates simultaneously (both above 0.5% per month), it reflects broad-based demand and supply expansion that strongly boosts corporate earnings and equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following joint acceleration triggers, mean 20-day SPX return is not positive (t-test one-sided p>0.05) or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL year-over-year below 2.5%), a decline in the federal funds rate of at least 25 basis points over 3 months signals preemptive easing that supports equity valuations via lower discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Conditional on CPIAUCSL below 2.5% YoY and FEDFUNDS trigger, the mean 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10) or the signal's long-only Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 over any 2-year window.
